title: Richard Buckminster Fuller & Anne Hewlett Fuller Dome Home (measured drawing sheet 1)
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey (HABS IL-1234), delineated by NPS/HABS
rightsholder_or_source: Library of Congress HABS (Prints & Photographs Division)
research_notes: Combined sheet: plan + roof plan + loft + elevation + section all on one drawing. This is the single drawing sheet (resource 0) of LoC item il0995 (HABS IL-1234); a data-pages PDF is the only other resource. Use the main-floor-plan quadrant at lower left.
research_features: Geodesic dome dwelling: the only measured-drawing sheet (sheet 1 of 1) combines a SITE PLAN / MAIN FLOOR PLAN (an octagonal interior layout inside the round dome with kitchen, living, master bedroom, entry terrace), plus a ROOF PLAN, LOFT PLAN, southwest elevation, and a full section. Excellent round/curved-shell test case where interior rooms are rectilinear inside a circular envelope.
feature_highlights: geodesic dome shell as the entire building envelope, with no conventional vertical exterior walls
rectilinear interior fitted inside a round, many-sided dome wall (rooms clipped/angled at the perimeter)
loft as a partial upper level inside the same dome volume
triangulated roof plan that is pure geodesic geometry
entry terrace as a roofed-but-open outdoor space rather than an enclosed room
ventilation alcove carved into the curved edge
dense composite HABS sheet: plan, roof plan, loft plan, elevation, section, details
site plan with property line, north arrow, and scale bars
supported_examples: rooms with names (entry, guest, bath, master bedroom, kitchen, living, dining)
custom-polygon room override for perimeter rooms clipped to straight facets
roadmap_examples: floor management for the loft as a partial upper level
site metadata for the surveyed property

gap_features.why: the building envelope is a geodesic dome shell with no vertical wall layout; a straight thickness-bearing wall model cannot represent the curved triangulated shell that defines the entire perimeter and roof
the geodesic roof and the curved shell over the living/loft volume are roof/sloped-ceiling geometry, not a flat per-room ceiling height
the entry terrace is a roofed-but-open outdoor space that must be drawable as a space without being a fully wall-enclosed room
